title: Exercises: Quantum Result Retrieval and Processing

exercises.title: Multi-Provider Result Parser
exercises.description: Build a unified result parser that handles different provider result formats.
tasks: Parse IBM Quantum result format (hex keys, nested structure)
Parse Amazon Braket result format (different field names)
Parse IonQ result format (probability-based)
Create unified QuantumResult output regardless of source
Handle provider-specific metadata extraction
exercises.expectedOutput: Unified parser handling IBM, Braket, and IonQ formats
hints: IBM uses hex strings (0x0, 0x3) that need binary conversion
IonQ may return probabilities - multiply by shots for counts

exercises.title: Statistical Analysis Suite
exercises.description: Implement comprehensive statistical analysis for quantum measurement results.
tasks: Calculate Shannon entropy of measurement distribution
Implement Wilson score confidence intervals
Compute KL divergence between measured and expected
Calculate statistical fidelity with an ideal state
Estimate minimum shots needed for target precision
exercises.starterCode:
import math
from typing import Dict, Tuple
import numpy as np

class QuantumStatistics:
    def __init__(self, counts: Dict[str, int], shots: int, num_qubits: int):
        self.counts = counts
        self.shots = shots
        self.num_qubits = num_qubits
        self.probs = {k: v/shots for k, v in counts.items()}
    
    def shannon_entropy(self) -> float:
        """Calculate Shannon entropy in bits."""
        # H = -sum(p * log2(p)) for p > 0
        pass
    
    def wilson_confidence_interval(self, outcome: str, 
                                   confidence: float = 0.95) -> Tuple[float, float]:
        """Wilson score interval for binomial proportion."""
        pass
    
    def kl_divergence(self, expected: Dict[str, float]) -> float:
        """Kullback-Leibler divergence from expected distribution."""
        # KL(P||Q) = sum(P(x) * log(P(x)/Q(x)))
        pass
    
    def state_fidelity(self, ideal_probs: Dict[str, float]) -> float:
        """Classical fidelity with ideal state."""
        # F = (sum(sqrt(p_i * q_i)))^2
        pass
    
    def required_shots(self, target_precision: float, 
                       confidence: float = 0.95) -> int:
        """Estimate shots needed for precision."""
        pass
    
    def is_uniform(self, significance: float = 0.05) -> bool:
        """Test if distribution is approximately uniform."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete statistical analysis toolkit for quantum results
hints: Use log2 for entropy in bits
Wilson interval handles edge cases better than normal approximation
Handle zero probabilities in KL divergence

exercises.title: Smart Result Cache
exercises.description: Implement an intelligent caching system with LRU eviction and compression.
tasks: Implement LRU (Least Recently Used) cache eviction
Add compression for large result storage
Implement cache warming from historical results
Add cache statistics (hit rate, miss rate, size)
Support cache invalidation patterns (by job, by backend, by time)
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import OrderedDict
import zlib
import pickle
from typing import Dict, Optional, Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
import time

@dataclass
class CacheStats:
    hits: int = 0
    misses: int = 0
    evictions: int = 0
    compressed_bytes: int = 0
    uncompressed_bytes: int = 0

class LRUResultCache:
    def __init__(self, max_size: int = 100, 
                 max_memory_mb: float = 100,
                 compression_threshold: int = 1000):
        self.max_size = max_size
        self.max_memory = max_memory_mb * 1024 * 1024
        self.compression_threshold = compression_threshold
        self._cache: OrderedDict = OrderedDict()
        self._stats = CacheStats()
    
    def get(self, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:
        """Get item, updating LRU order."""
        pass
    
    def set(self, key: str, value: Any, ttl: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
        """Set item with optional TTL."""
        pass
    
    def _evict_if_needed(self) -> None:
        """Evict oldest items if over capacity."""
        pass
    
    def _compress(self, data: bytes) -> bytes:
        """Compress data if over threshold."""
        pass
    
    def _decompress(self, data: bytes, compressed: bool) -> bytes:
        """Decompress data if needed."""
        pass
    
    def invalidate_pattern(self, pattern: str) -> int:
        """Invalidate all keys matching pattern."""
        pass
    
    def warm_cache(self, results: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """Pre-populate cache with known results."""
        pass
    
    def get_stats(self) -> CacheStats:
        """Return cache statistics."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: LRU cache with compression and statistics
hints: OrderedDict maintains insertion order for LRU
Move accessed items to end to mark as recently used

exercises.title: Result Aggregation Pipeline
exercises.description: Build a pipeline for aggregating and analyzing results from distributed quantum experiments.
tasks: Aggregate counts from multiple jobs running same circuit
Compute weighted averages based on shot counts
Detect and flag outlier results
Implement bootstrapping for error estimation
Generate summary reports with confidence intervals
exercises.starterCode:
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class AggregatedResult:
    combined_counts: Dict[str, int]
    total_shots: int
    num_jobs: int
    probabilities: Dict[str, float]
    confidence_intervals: Dict[str, Tuple[float, float]]
    outlier_jobs: List[str]
    
class ResultAggregator:
    def __init__(self, outlier_threshold: float = 3.0):
        self.outlier_threshold = outlier_threshold  # Z-score threshold
    
    def aggregate(self, results: List[Tuple[str, Dict[str, int], int]]) \
            -> AggregatedResult:
        """Aggregate multiple results. Each tuple is (job_id, counts, shots)."""
        pass
    
    def weighted_probability(self, results: List[Tuple[Dict[str, int], int]],
                            outcome: str) -> float:
        """Compute shot-weighted probability for outcome."""
        pass
    
    def detect_outliers(self, results: List[Tuple[str, Dict[str, int], int]]) \
            -> List[str]:
        """Detect outlier jobs using Z-score on probabilities."""
        pass
    
    def bootstrap_confidence(self, counts: Dict[str, int], 
                            shots: int,
                            n_bootstrap: int = 1000,
                            confidence: float = 0.95) -> Dict[str, Tuple[float, float]]:
        """Bootstrap confidence intervals for each outcome."""
        pass
    
    def generate_report(self, aggregated: AggregatedResult) -> str:
        """Generate human-readable summary report."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete aggregation pipeline with outlier detection and bootstrapping
hints: Weight by shots: sum(count_i * weight_i) / sum(weight_i)
